sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard (formerly CAC/RS 103 -1978) shall apply to quick-frozen blueberries of the species Vaccinium corymbosum L.i,ii, Vaccinium angustifolium Ait.iii and Vaccinium ashei READE,iv as defined below and offered for direct consumption without further processing, except for repacking, if required. It does not apply to the product when indicated as intended for further processing or for other industrial purposes, nor to bilberriesv as covered by the Standard for Quick-Frozen Bilberries (CXS 76-1981).1

sections.title: Product definition
sections.text: Quick-frozen blueberries are the product prepared from fresh, clean, sound, ripe and stemmed blueberries of firm texture, conforming to the characteristics of Vaccinium corymbosum L., Vaccinium angustifolium Ait. and Vaccinium ashei READE, and which are packed with or without a dry sugar or a sugar syrup and frozen in an appropriate manner.

sections.title: Process definition
sections.text: Quick-frozen blueberries are the product subjected to a freezing process in appropriate equipment and complying with the conditions laid down hereafter. This freezing operation shall be carried out in such a way that the range of temperature of maximum crystallization is passed quickly. The quick-freezing process shall not be regarded as complete unless and until the product temperature has reached -18 6C (0 6F) at the thermal centre after thermal stabilization. The recognized practice of repacking quick-frozen products under controlled conditions is permitted.

sections.title: Handling practice
sections.text: The product shall be handled under such conditions as will maintain the quality during transportation, storage and distribution up to and including the time of final sale. It is recommended that during storage, transportation, distribution and retail, the product be handled in accordance with the provisions in the Code of Practice for the Processing and Handling of Quick-Frozen Foods (CXC 8-1976).2

sections.title: Definition of "defective" for quality factors
sections.text:
Any sample unit taken in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL 6.5, (see relevant Codex texts on methods of analysis and sampling) shall be regarded as a "defective" for the respective characteristics, as follows:
(a) that exceeds the tolerance for mineral impurities in Section 3.3.2;
(b) that exceeds the tolerance for "free-flowing" in Section 3.3.3; or
(c) that exceeds the "maximum total allowable points for visual defects" in any one or more of the categories in Table 1 (3.3.4.3); or that exceeds the tolerance for disintegrated, badly crushed, or smashed in Table 1 (3.3.4.3).

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Packaging used for quick-frozen blueberries shall: (a) protect the organoleptic and other quality characteristics of the product; (b) protect the product against microbiological and other contamination; (c) protect the product from dehydration and, where appropriate, leakage as far as technologically practicable; and (d) do not pass on to the product any odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics throughout the processing (where applicable) and distribution of the product up to the time of final sale.

sentences.original: Quick-frozen blueberries are the product prepared from fresh, clean, sound, ripe and stemmed blueberries of firm texture, conforming to the characteristics of Vaccinium corymbosum L., Vaccinium angustifolium Ait. and Vaccinium ashei READE, and which are packed with or without a dry sugar or a sugar syrup and frozen in an appropriate manner.

definitions.Extraneous vegetable material (EVM): leaves or portions of the blueberry plant, or other similar vegetable material which is harmless
definitions.Cap stems (stalks): the immediate stem that attaches the blueberry to the plant, whether or not attached to the berry, and which is 2 mm, or greater in length
definitions.Unripe berries: completely green berries or berries that have a green cast that predominates over the normal reddish-purple colour of blueberries
definitions.Dissimilar varieties: other edible berries that are distinctly different in colour or shape, which have definitely different internal characteristics than blueberries
definitions.Blemished: blueberries which show visible signs of damage by insect s or by pathological or mechanical injury
definitions.Undeveloped or mummified: berries that are badly shrivelled, dried or hard

classification.justification: The sentence provides multiple term definitions, a descriptive pattern not normative in modality.

sentences.original:
Any sample unit taken in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL 6.5, (see relevant Codex texts on methods of analysis and sampling) shall be regarded as a "defective" for the respective characteristics, as follows:
(a) that exceeds the tolerance for mineral impurities in Section 3.3.2;
(b) that exceeds the tolerance for "free-flowing" in Section 3.3.3; or
(c) that exceeds the "maximum total allowable points for visual defects" in any one or more of the categories in Table 1 (3.3.4.3); or that exceeds the tolerance for disintegrated, badly crushed, or smashed in Table 1 (3.3.4.3).

pages.summary: Página contém o título principal do documento como nível 1 e um cabeçalho com código e número do padrão. Texto interno está associado ao título principal.

Página com cabeçalho contendo código e número do padrão. Documento não apresenta título principal visível. Contém uma seção de emenda de 2022 em nível 1 com texto explicativo. Sem continuidade de página anterior.

Página analisa as seções 1 a 3 do documento. A página contém títulos hierarquizados de nível 1 a 3, com subseções e texto associado. O cabeçalho indica o código e página. A seção 2 continua na próxima página. Não há título principal do documento visível. Sem conteúdo vindo da página anterior.

A página contém a continuação da seção 3.3 iniciada anteriormente, com múltiplas subseções do nível 2 e 3 claramente estruturadas e tituladas. O cabeçalho da página indica código e número da página. A seção 3.2.4 inicia e termina nesta página. O texto está associado corretamente aos respectivos títulos numerados, sem inclusão do cabeçalho repetitivo. As marcações de continuidade indicam que os itens em 3.3 vieram da página anterior, mas não continuam para a próxima.

A página contém principalmente subseções numeradas no nível 2 e 3, com um destaque para a seção detalhada 3.3.4.3 que continua da página anterior. As seções 3.4, 4 e 5 são claramente tituladas e apresentam texto conciso associado. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e removido do texto dos nós. Nenhuma seção continua para a próxima página.

Esta página contém principalmente seções numeradas nos níveis 1, 2 e 3, com destaque para a seção 6 LABELLING e suas subseções detalhadas. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e excluído dos conteúdos textuais. Não há indicações de continuidade de seção da página anterior nem para a próxima.
Página contém uma seção principal 'NOTES' em nível 1 com textos referentes a referências da FAO e WHO. Não há subdivisões nesta página. Sem continuidade entre páginas.
